text.title: Benefits Realization Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the benefits-realization methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the final rule defines when to skip this methodology entirely.

rule.statement: Each benefit MUST have a named business-stakeholder owner — never the PM and never 'the team'.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Baseline measurement MUST be captured BEFORE go-live; post-hoc baselines are reconstructions, not measurements.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Outputs (deliverables) MUST be distinguished from outcomes (behaviour change) from benefits (measured improvement); reporting MUST track all three separately.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Each benefit MUST have ≥5 measurement points (M1, M3, M6, Y1, Y2) to detect decay.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: RAG MUST use fixed thresholds (Green ≥90% of expected, Yellow 70-90%, Red <70%) — never narrative-only colour.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Skip when no quantitative benefit claim, no owner, or project <$50k.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.
